title: Table: The table shows information about medical care in three different European count...
description: The table shows information about medical care in three different European countries between 1980 and 2000. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

1. Hiểu đề

Bảng so sánh 3 chỉ số y tế (physicians, hospital beds, length of stay) tại Austria, France, Sweden qua 1980–2000

2. Introduction

The table compares three healthcare indicators in Austria, France and Sweden between 1980 and 2000.

3. Overview

Sweden luôn cao nhất ở beds và length of stay; France giảm rõ về physicians và thời gian nằm viện; Austria tăng mạnh beds và biến động lớn ở length of stay

4. Body

Body 1 (Physicians \+ Beds):

Physicians: Sweden cao nhất (3.6 → 3.8), France giảm mạnh (2.4 → 1.3), Austria tăng nhẹ (0.8 → 1)

Beds: Sweden ổn định cao (6.4 → 6.9), Austria tăng mạnh (1.4 → 3.7), France cũng tăng (0.2 → 1.6)

Body 2 (Length of stay):

Austria biến động mạnh (18 → 6 → 18); France giảm đều (9 → 5); Sweden giảm rồi tăng nhẹ (23 → 18 → 21)

5. Key phrases

remained the highest; rose sharply; declined steadily; fluctuated significantly; by contrast

The table compares three healthcare indicators, namely physicians and hospital beds per 1,000 people and the average length of hospital stay, in Austria, France and Sweden from 1980 to 2000.

Overall, Sweden consistently recorded the highest figures for hospital beds and length of stay, while France showed clear declines in both the number of physicians and the duration of hospital stays. By contrast, Austria experienced a significant rise in hospital beds and notable fluctuations in length of stay.

In terms of physicians, Sweden remained the highest throughout, increasing slightly from 3.6 in 1980 to 3.8 in 2000 despite a dip in 1990. France, on the other hand, declined steadily from 2.4 to 1.3, whereas Austria rose modestly from 0.8 to 1. Regarding hospital beds, Sweden maintained a consistently high level, rising marginally from 6.4 to 6.9. Austria saw a sharp increase from 1.4 to 3.7, while France also grew from 0.2 to 1.6, although it remained the lowest.

As for the average length of hospital stay, Austria fluctuated significantly, dropping from 18 days to 6 before returning to 18. France declined steadily from 9 to 5, while Sweden fell from 23 to 18 before rising slightly to 21.
